resources.string: Select your role on the court to personalize your training
Team organizer. Ball handling, vision and speed.
Outside scorer. Shooting and driving to the basket.
Versatile player. Shooting, rebounding and perimeter defense.
Inside and outside game. Strength and mid-range shooting.
Dominates under the rim. Post play, rebounding and interior defense.
Dribble the ball in a figure eight pattern between your legs at ankle level. Keep your back straight and use your fingertips. Do 3 sets of 30 seconds. Improves ball control and coordination.
No videos available for this role
Practice dribbling alternating right and left hand. Keep your head up and eyes forward. Do 3 sets of 30 seconds per hand.
Place your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees, align your elbow with the basket and release the ball with a smooth wrist motion. Do 20 repetitions.
Adopt the defensive stance with bent knees, straight back and extended arms. Move laterally following the attacker without crossing your feet.
Hold the ball with both hands at chest height. Extend your arms toward your teammate and release the ball with a wrist snap. Do 3 sets of 15 repetitions.
Reference video with fundamental point guard movements: ball handling on the move, direction changes, court vision and quick decision making.
Coordinate with the center to use the screen. After the screen, decide whether to drive, shoot or pass to the roller based on the defense reaction.
Alternate between fast and slow dribble to unbalance the defender. Use the speed change when the defender drops their guard.

From the defensive stance, practice quick lateral movements of 4-5 meters in each direction without crossing your feet. React by changing direction. Work on anticipation to intercept the opponent\'s dribble. 3 sets of 45 seconds.

Position yourself half an arm\'s length from the attacker with your active hand on the ball. Without fouling, maintain pressure to disrupt the dribble and pass. The goal is to force errors and slow down the opponent\'s game.
From the three-point line, take 1-2 dribbles toward the basket and finish with a jump shot in the mid-range area. Work on balance and elevation before releasing the ball.

Practice reading the defense after a screen: if the defender goes behind the screen, shoot three; if they get caught, drive; if there\'s defensive help, pass to the screener. Train quick decision-making.

While dribbling with your head up, identify teammates on the perimeter and deliver the pass at the right moment without telegraphing your intent. A point guard who doesn\'t look at the ball has an advantage over the defense.
From the three-point line, drive to the basket avoiding the defender. Finish with a layup using the hand furthest from the defender. Practice from both sides.
Dribbling on the move and shooting guard drills
Dribbling drills combined with shooting guard technical moves: crossover, between the legs, behind the back and finishes at the rim.
Prepare your shot before receiving the ball. As soon as you catch it, shoot directly without extra dribbles. The goal is to reduce your release time.
Fake going one direction and cut sharply toward the rim or open space. The goal is to get open from your defender to receive the pass.
On a drive, shoot a soft shot over the interior defender when you can\'t reach the rim with a regular layup. Practice from both sides.
Work on three-point shooting from both corners after receiving a pass. Corners are the most efficient spots to shoot threes. Practice catch and shoot and shooting after an adjustment step.
From the low post, react to a perimeter pass and close out quickly on the shooter with arms up. Work on controlled stopping and balance at the end without fouling.
Practice following the attacker in their off-ball movements using your body and peripheral vision. Anticipate cuts to the rim to never lose sight of the opponent or the ball.
From the defensive stance, practice quick lateral movements of 4-5 meters in each direction without crossing your feet. React by changing direction. 3 sets of 45 seconds.
From the three-point line, take 1-2 dribbles toward the basket and finish with a jump shot in the mid-range area. Work on balance and elevation before releasing the ball.
Reference video with key small forward movements: mid-range shooting, off-ball movement, cuts to the rim, offensive rebounding and perimeter defense.
Anticipate the shot trajectory and position yourself between the defender and the rim. Jump with both feet and catch the ball at its highest point.
Work on defensive positioning on the perimeter. Keep proper distance from the attacker and react to both the dribble and the pass.

From the triple threat position (dribble, shoot or pass possible), practice reading the defense and deciding in less than 2 seconds. If the defender is close, shoot; if they back off, drive; if there\'s help defense, pass.
Drive to the rim forcing defensive help and at the last moment pass to the open teammate on the perimeter. Work on peripheral vision during the drive and precise passing under pressure.
Practice getting open using an off-ball screen from a teammate. After the screen, decide between coming off to shoot, cutting to the rim or spacing away to open up the floor.
The small forward must defend both perimeter and interior players. Practice switching assignments adapting your body position depending on whether the opponent is quicker or more physical.
From the low post, react to a perimeter pass and close out quickly on the shooter with arms up. Work on controlled stopping and balance at the end without fouling.
From the high post or baseline, work on mid-range shooting after one dribble entry. Practice with and without a defender from both sides.
Post moves adapted to the modern power forward: spins, finishes near the rim, footwork and how to gain advantage without being the tallest player.
After the opponent\'s shot, box out the attacker and jump for the ball. Catch with two hands and protect it from the opponent on landing.
Work the three-point shot as an additional weapon. Practice from the corners and from the top, catching in motion.
Position yourself with feet shoulder-width apart to screen the defender off your teammate. Hold the contact until the point guard clears the screen and roll to the rim or open space.
Work on pivot turns from the high and low post: face-up turn, drop step and crossover step. Each move should end in a finish near the rim or a mid-range shot.
Position yourself behind the attacker in the low post using your body to push them out of their favorite spot. Keep your arms active to disrupt the pass reception before they receive the ball.
The modern power forward must also defend on the perimeter. Practice closing out on three-point shooters with a short sprint followed by a controlled stop with arms up.
Anticipate the shot trajectory and position yourself between the defender and the rim. Jump with both feet and catch the ball at its highest point. Protect it upon landing.
From the high post, work on mid-range shooting after one dribble entry. Practice from both sides of the post with and without a defender.
Reference video with essential center moves under the rim: back-to-basket post play, hook shots with both hands and finishes close to the basket.
From the low post, take a lateral step away from the defender and release the ball with an arcing arm motion over the defender.
Work on defensive zone positioning. Keep your arms up, anticipate the pass and jump to block without committing a personal foul.

From the low post with your back to the rim, take a long step toward the basket on the defender\'s weak side, pivot and finish with a layup or short shot. It\'s the most effective offensive move for a center near the rim.
Practice the center\'s role in the pick and roll: set the screen correctly, roll to the rim or open space after contact and receive the pass from the point guard to finish near the rim.

Practice block timing by waiting for the moment the attacker releases the ball. The goal is to block the shot vertically without invading the opponent\'s space. A bad block means a foul; a good block changes the game.
Work on positioning behind and in front of the opposing center depending on the situation. In front to deny the pass; behind to anticipate the rebound. Use your body to hold position without fouling.
After the opponent\'s shot, box out the attacker with your body and jump for the ball. Catch with two hands and protect it from the opponent upon landing.
Work on movement inside the zone to anticipate interior passes. Keep your arms up, your feet active and communicate with teammates to cover spaces without leaving gaps in the paint.
You have no favorite drills yet
Do you want to change your position on the court?
An error occurred. Please try again.
Complete advanced training session for point guards: high-speed ball handling, explosive direction changes and decision-making under defensive pressure.
Specific drills for shooting guards attacking the rim: drives, finishes through contact and floaters from both sides.
Advanced training for shooting guards: attack moves combined with dribbling, reading the defense and varied finishes.
Offensive drills designed for small forwards: cuts to the rim, mid-range shooting, drives and off-ball movement to get open.
Advanced training for small forwards: combination of offensive and defensive skills, game reading and versatility in different situations.
Basic post drills: positioning, receiving the ball, simple pivots and finishes near the rim for interior players.
Advanced post moves: chaining fakes, hooks, drop steps and finishes through contact for high-level interior players.
